topic: Ionic Product of Water (Kw) & pH Scale
description: Two-curve plot: Kw rising with T; pH of pure water falling with T. Marks 25°C (pH 7) and 100°C (pH ~6).
Two side-by-side cartoons: LEFT "Add H+" caught by CH3COO-. RIGHT "Add OH-" caught by CH3COOH. Arrows showing equilibrium shifts.
notes: Buffer action is #1 stumble; picture nails it.

description: Vertical bar chart: log[S2-] thresholds for CuS, HgS, PbS, ZnS, MnS. Horizontal lines showing [S2-] in acidic vs basic H2S, intersecting different bars.
notes: Underpins qualitative analysis — visual very high leverage.

description: pH vs volume curve: starts ~1, climbs gently, sharp vertical rise at equivalence point pH 7, plateaus near 13.
pH vs volume for weak-strong: starts ~3, buffer plateau at pKa, jump to equivalence at ~8.5, levels at ~12.
